model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the CLICK ME button
Previous iterations:
  iter 0: at (184,426) said dx=70 dy=-30 conf=0.80 -> (254,396)
Call report_offset().

raw_message.content:
The previous iteration moved the click position to (254,396) with a confidence of 0.80. The image shows that the red crosshair, which represents the current predicted click position, is to the left and slightly above the "CLICK ME" button. To center the click on the button, we need to move the click position to the right and slightly down. Based on the visual alignment, a dx of 40 and a dy of 10 seems reasonable. The confidence can be increased to 0.9 as the target is becoming clearer.

Call report_offset(dx=40, dy=10, confidence=0.9).
